name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international pawn shop operator with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans, retail sales, and gold buying.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the largest international pawn store chain in North America and Latin America. The company was founded to serve cash and credit-constrained consumers through a network of retail pawn locations and complementary financial services. FirstCash is publicly traded and included in both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, reflecting its scale and market significance in the consumer finance sector.

FirstCash's primary business model revolves around pawn loans secured by pledged personal property—a non-recourse lending product where customers pledge items like jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments to receive immediate cash. Beyond pawn loans, the company operates retail sales of merchandise, gold and precious metal buying services, layaway plans (with 10% down payment option), and through its subsidiary AFF, lease-to-own and retail finance solutions across 15,000+ merchant locations. The company employs approximately 22,000 people across the U.S., Latin America, and the UK.

What distinguishes FirstCash is its massive geographic footprint—over 3,300 pawn stores spanning 29 U.S. states plus Washington D.C., the entire United Kingdom, and multiple Latin American countries including all Mexican states, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador. This international scale and diversified revenue streams (pawn loans, retail merchandise, gold buying, layaway services, and payment solutions through AFF) set it apart from smaller regional pawn operators. The company also maintains a comprehensive online store locator and inventory system, indicating operational sophistication beyond typical pawn shops.

FirstCash serves a legitimate financial need for consumers requiring immediate cash without credit checks, though pawn loans typically carry high interest rates inherent to the collateral-based lending model. The non-recourse nature of pawn loans (you lose the item but not additional money) provides consumer protection compared to other secured lending products. However, customers should understand that pawn loans are short-term solutions, not credit-building products, and losing pledged items means losing personal property of sentimental or practical value.
pros: Immediate cash availability with no credit check or approval process required
Non-recourse loans mean you cannot lose more than the pledged item—no deficiency judgments
Massive network of 3,300+ stores across 29 U.S. states, UK, and Latin America for convenience
Buys and sells diverse merchandise categories (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, appliances)
Offers layaway plans with affordable 10% down payment for alternative purchasing
Gold and precious metal buying service for quick liquidation of valuables
Publicly traded company with transparent financial reporting and regulatory oversight
Long operating history as leading international pawn operator with 22,000 employees
cons: Pawn loans carry high interest rates typical of collateral-based lending products
You permanently lose pledged personal property if you cannot repay the loan and interest
Not a credit-building solution—pawn activity does not report to credit bureaus
Limited loan terms (typically 30-90 days) require quick repayment or item forfeiture
Loan amounts restricted to a percentage of item's resale value, limiting funds availability
best_for: Consumers needing immediate emergency cash without credit qualification or waiting periods
People with items of value (jewelry, electronics, tools) they can temporarily part with
Individuals with poor credit or no credit history who cannot access traditional loans
Those seeking short-term bridging loans until next paycheck or income arrives
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers facing immediate cash shortages who have valuable personal property to pledge and can repay within typical 30-90 day pawn loan windows. The critical caveat is that pawn loans are short-term emergency solutions only—failure to repay means permanent loss of the pledged item, and the high interest rates make this an expensive financing option compared to personal loans or lines of credit for those who qualify.
services: Pawn loans secured by pledged personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, instruments, sporting goods)
Retail sales of merchandise from inventory
Gold, silver, and precious metal buying and liquidation
Layaway plans with 10% down payment option
Store locator service to find nearest location among 3,300+ stores
Lease-to-own payment solutions through subsidiary AFF (15,000+ merchant partners)
Retail finance payment solutions through AFF network
Cash payment on the spot for items purchased
